channel.title: NPR Topics: Health
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump pulls Casey Means' stalled surgeon general nomination, announces new pick
item.description: President Trump says he's nominating former Fox News Channel contributor Dr. Nicole Saphier for surgeon general after Dr. Casey Means' path forward stalled in the Senate over questions about her experience and her stance on vaccines.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump says he's nominating former Fox News Channel contributor Dr. Nicole Saphier for surgeon general after Dr. Casey Means' path forward stalled in the Senate over questions about her experience and her stance on vaccines.

(Image credit: Tom Brenner)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: In real-world test, an AI model did better than ER doctors at diagnosing patients
item.description: Researchers evaluated how well an AI model could diagnose and make decisions about patient care.
item.content:encoded:
Researchers evaluated how well an AI model could diagnose and make decisions about patient care.

(Image credit: shapecharge/E+)
item.dc:creator: Will Stone

item.title: To catch colorectal cancer early, advocates push to make 'poop talk' OK
item.description: It's a very treatable form of cancer if caught early, yet younger adults rarely get screened. Patient advocates want more people to talk to their doctors about risk factors and number two.
item.content:encoded: It's a very treatable form of cancer if caught early, yet younger adults rarely get screened. Patient advocates want more people to talk to their doctors about risk factors and number two.
item.dc:creator: Yuki Noguchi

item.title: RFK pushes abstinence and wellness programs he says helped him recover from addiction
item.description: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy is promoting abstinence and 12-step addiction treatment programs that he says helped him recover from heroin use.
item.content:encoded: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy is promoting abstinence and 12-step addiction treatment programs that he says helped him recover from heroin use.
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann

item.title: A look at one of the farms RFK Jr. has pointed to as a model for 'reparenting'
item.description: The U.S. health secretary says he wants to shift addiction care toward an approach that includes rural farms or camps for people in recovery. Many addiction experts say the idea is outdated.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. health secretary says he wants to shift addiction care toward an approach that includes rural farms or camps for people in recovery. Many addiction experts say the idea is outdated.
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann

item.title: Long a dream, it's now real: a fast and accurate TB test that doesn't need phlegm
item.description: TB tests use phlegm — not the easiest thing to get or work with. It takes time for results. And there can be false negatives and positives. A new test is more accurate and takes less than half an hour.
item.content:encoded:
TB tests use phlegm — not the easiest thing to get or work with. It takes time for results. And there can be false negatives and positives. A new test is more accurate and takes less than half an hour.

(Image credit: BSIP/Universal Images Group via)
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: The Trump team is quietly eliminating U.S. support for birth control abroad
item.description: Congress has allocated more than $500 million for family planning work internationally. The Trump administration hasn't spent it — and the consequences are already being felt.
item.content:encoded:
Congress has allocated more than $500 million for family planning work internationally. The Trump administration hasn't spent it — and the consequences are already being felt.

(Image credit: Edward Echwalu for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel

item.title: Welcome to 'Anxietyland' theme park, where the rides are no fun
item.description: From the Emotional Roller Coaster to the Worry-go-round, cartoonist Gemma Correll walks us through her brain's not-so-amusing amusement park in a darkly funny memoir.
item.content:encoded: From the Emotional Roller Coaster to the Worry-go-round, cartoonist Gemma Correll walks us through her brain's not-so-amusing amusement park in a darkly funny memoir.
item.dc:creator: LA Johnson

item.title: Baby teeth hold clues to the harms of toxic metals for infants — and older kids
item.description: By analyzing layers in these teeth, scientists have pinpointed a critical window when baby brains are most vulnerable to toxic metals — and linked that to behavior problems later in life.
item.content:encoded: By analyzing layers in these teeth, scientists have pinpointed a critical window when baby brains are most vulnerable to toxic metals — and linked that to behavior problems later in life.
item.dc:creator: Maria Godoy

item.title: How a Supreme Court fight over fish oil could raise your prescription drug costs
item.description: The justices are set to hear Hikma v. Amarin, a battle over drug patents that could raise costs for patients and change the way generic companies do business.
item.content:encoded: The justices are set to hear Hikma v. Amarin, a battle over drug patents that could raise costs for patients and change the way generic companies do business.
item.dc:creator: Leslie Walker
